issues.description: The Telegram webhook handler at /webhook/telegram has a security bypass: if TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET is not set, ALL requests are accepted with no authentication whatsoever. Line: `if expected_secret and secret != expected_secret`. An attacker who knows the endpoint can send arbitrary /ack_alert commands to silence monitoring alerts.

The /api/scan endpoint fires asyncio.create_task(_run()) and immediately returns {ok: True} — but _run() is never awaited and exceptions are silently swallowed. Scanner errors produce no feedback to the caller and no logging path is visible.

_telegram_send() uses urllib.request.urlopen synchronously inside an async FastAPI handler (the /webhook/telegram route). This blocks the event loop for up to 8 seconds (the timeout) on every Telegram reply, degrading all concurrent request handling.

claude-agent-sdk>=0.1 is listed as a dependency but the CLAUDE.md states 'TAO_USE_AGENT_SDK=0 causes an ImportError at startup (deliberate)' and sdk-version-policy.md references it as the sole execution path since RA-576. The package name 'claude-agent-sdk' does not match the Anthropic SDK package 'anthropic' — this appears to be a fictional/misnamed package that would fail pip install in CI and production.

WebSocket handler /ws/build/{sid} accepts authentication via Authorization header or cookie but the header name 'authorization' is checked with .replace('Bearer ', '') — this is case-sensitive and will silently fail for clients sending 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' with capital A, rejecting valid tokens.

The /api/sessions/{sid}/logs SSE endpoint has no authentication dependency — unlike all other /api/* routes which use Depends(require_auth), this endpoint is unauthenticated. Build logs stream to any unauthenticated caller who knows a session ID.

The /webhook/telegram endpoint is not rate-limited (no Depends(require_rate_limit)), unlike all other webhook endpoints. A flood of Telegram webhook calls will consume unbounded event loop time, especially given the synchronous _telegram_send() blocking issue.

The _resilient() wrapper restarts crashed background tasks after a 10s delay with no exponential backoff and no maximum retry cap. A task that crashes on startup (e.g., linear_todo_poller with missing LINEAR_API_KEY) will crash-restart in a tight loop forever, generating log spam and masking the root cause.

The build_parallel fan-out endpoint calls await fan_out(...) synchronously in the request handler without a timeout. A long-running fan-out blocks the request for the full duration. All other build endpoints use BackgroundTasks or asyncio.create_task for deferred execution.

The Telegram bot token (8630069375:AAH5CDnCm5ApdFsdfFz1KwNGEFDtgy9yvDA) appears verbatim in .harness/RA-588-PREFLIGHT.md. Regardless of gitignore rules on .env files, this specific token is committed to a .harness/ markdown file which IS tracked in git. The bot token is live and should be rotated immediately.

Telegram user ID (8792816988) and bot token (8630069375:AAH5CDnCm5ApdFsdfFz1KwNGEFDtgy9yvDA) are both committed in plaintext in a tracked .harness/ file. The .gitignore explicitly excludes .harness/telegram-inbox/ but not .harness/*.md files.

CSP header in SecurityHeaders middleware allows 'unsafe-inline' for both script-src and style-src. This nullifies XSS protection for inline scripts. Combined with the unvalidated inputs flowing through the build system, this is a meaningful attack surface reduction failure.

The /api/build/parallel endpoint has Depends(require_auth) and Depends(require_rate_limit) as route-level dependencies, but they are passed as a list in the decorator — `dependencies=[Depends(require_auth), Depends(require_rate_limit)]`. This is correct FastAPI syntax, but the parallel build creates N worker sessions in a single call. TAO_MAX_SESSIONS rate limiting applies per-session but not per-fan-out call, allowing a single authenticated user to exhaust the session pool with one request.

The @app.on_event('startup') decorator is used twice (once in on_startup and once in _start_claude_poll). FastAPI deprecated on_event in favor of lifespan context managers. More critically, having two startup handlers makes initialization order implicit and fragile.

The /api/monitor endpoint fires background_tasks.add_task(_run) where _run is an async def that calls run_monitor_cycle() — but BackgroundTasks expects a regular callable, not an async one. The async def _run will be called but not awaited by BackgroundTasks, meaning it runs as a coroutine object and does nothing. This is a silent no-op.

The 'dev' script sources .env.local via bash: `bash -c 'set -a && source .env.local && set +a && next dev'`. This is Linux/macOS only and silently breaks on Windows despite the README specifying Windows PowerShell setup. The CI workflow uses ubuntu-latest so this mismatch is masked.

lucide-react is pinned at ^1.8.0 but lucide-react's latest stable releases are in the 0.x range (e.g., 0.462). Version 1.8.0 does not exist in the npm registry — this will fail `npm ci` in CI and on fresh installs.

The /api/lessons GET endpoint accepts a `limit` query parameter with `min(limit, 200)` but no minimum validation. A caller can pass limit=0 or limit=-1 and receive an empty or undefined response without error.

The gap audit JSON itself documents that build log SSE streaming is claimed complete in spec.md but missing from main.py. However, the provided main.py DOES include a GET /api/sessions/{sid}/logs SSE endpoint — the gap audit is outdated/wrong. This demonstrates the self-assessment reliability problem documented in the board minutes.

Two Dockerfiles exist (root Dockerfile and app/Dockerfile) with different WORKDIR, COPY patterns, and CMD paths. The root Dockerfile copies app/server/ to ./app/server/ and runs 'app.server.main:app'. The app/Dockerfile copies server/ to ./server/ and runs 'server.main:app'. These will produce containers that break if the wrong one is used — no single source of truth for the build artifact.

The health endpoint imports time, shutil at function call time rather than at module level. These are stdlib imports — importing them inside the function on every /health call is unnecessary overhead, especially for a health check that should be as fast as possible.

The _find_active_session_for_repo() helper iterates _sessions.values() linearly on every webhook call. With hundreds of sessions this is O(n). A secondary index from repo_url to session_id would be O(1).

The anthropic-docs files contain raw Next.js RSC payload (self.__next_f.push([1,...]) serialized JavaScript), not readable documentation. The fetch captured the client-side rendered HTML including JavaScript chunks rather than the actual doc content. These files are effectively useless as knowledge base entries.

missingTests: Authentication bypass paths — no test for the Telegram webhook with missing TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET accepting arbitrary requests
SSE /api/sessions/{sid}/logs endpoint — no authentication test, no test for cursor-based pagination correctness
WebSocket authentication — case-sensitivity of Authorization header not tested
Rate limiting enforcement — no test that the 30 req/min limit actually blocks requests
The _resilient() wrapper crash-restart behavior — no test for infinite restart loops
Fan-out parallelism session pool exhaustion — no test that build_parallel cannot exhaust TAO_MAX_SESSIONS in one call
Webhook duplicate detection — no test for _find_active_session_for_repo() race condition between two simultaneous webhook deliveries
cron trigger firing with bogus last_fired_at timestamps — the scheduler debounce bug documented in lessons is untested
Session resume from phase checkpoint — no integration test for the POST /api/sessions/{sid}/resume endpoint
The /api/monitor BackgroundTasks async-def no-op bug — no test verifies run_monitor_cycle actually executes
Input validation edge cases on /api/lessons — limit=0, limit=-1, empty lesson string
Telegram alert acknowledgment (mark_alert_acked) — no test for the Supabase write path
Pipeline ship phase hard gate — no test for the run_ship_phase blocking behavior
securityConcerns: COMMITTED SECRET: .harness/RA-588-PREFLIGHT.md contains a live Telegram bot token (8630069375:AAH5CDnCm5ApdFsdfFz1KwNGEFDtgy9yvDA) and user ID (8792816988) in a tracked git file. This must be rotated immediately.
UNAUTHENTICATED ENDPOINT: /api/sessions/{sid}/logs (app/server/main.py) streams build logs to any caller — no require_auth dependency unlike all other /api/* endpoints.

WEBHOOK SECRET BYPASS: /webhook/telegram in app/server/main.py accepts all requests when TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var is unset, with no warning at startup. Combined with the /ack_alert handler, this allows unauthenticated alert silencing.

SYNCHRONOUS HTTP IN ASYNC HANDLER: _telegram_send() uses urllib.request.urlopen with timeout=8 in app/server/main.py — blocks the event loop, enabling a DoS by causing 8-second stalls on every Telegram webhook delivery.
CSP UNSAFE-INLINE: SecurityHeaders middleware in app/server/main.py sets script-src 'unsafe-inline' and style-src 'unsafe-inline', negating XSS protection from Content-Security-Policy.

SESSION POOL EXHAUSTION: /api/build/parallel in app/server/main.py allows a single authenticated request to create up to 8 concurrent sessions (n_workers max), potentially consuming the entire TAO_MAX_SESSIONS=3 pool multiple times over with one call.

ARBITRARY COMMAND ALLOWLIST: .claude/settings.json uses defaultMode: bypassPermissions with broad Bash allowlists including Bash(rm:*), Bash(mv:*), Bash(curl:*), Bash(wget:*). Combined with Bash(git:*) and Write(*), a compromised session has near-complete filesystem access.

SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY in .env.example: The file documents SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY which bypasses Row Level Security — if this value leaks from a Railway environment, the entire Supabase database is exposed with no RLS protection.
positives: Pydantic field validators on all request models in main.py with specific allowlists (repo_url prefix check, model enum, worker count bounds) — clean input validation at the API boundary
HMAC timing-safe comparison for both GitHub and Linear webhook signatures using hmac.compare_digest — correct implementation that avoids timing oracle attacks

Session token uses HttpOnly + SameSite cookies with conditional Secure/SameSite=None based on cloud detection — correct handling of the cross-origin cookie problem for Railway + Vercel split deployment
The _resilient() background task wrapper pattern — crash recovery with logging is the right approach for background pollers, even if the backoff needs improvement
write-to-.tmp-then-os.replace() atomic write pattern documented in CLAUDE.md — correct approach for session persistence that survives crashes

The .harness/ architecture as a persistent, git-tracked knowledge base for agent decisions, lessons, and charters is genuinely well-designed — provides durable cross-session context that most agent systems lack
Three-tier CI pipeline (python + frontend + smoke-prod) with post-deploy gates against production Railway URL — production readiness verification is baked into the deployment workflow
Comprehensive .gitignore protecting all .env variants, runtime data, and credential files — the gitignore is thorough and correctly structured
The ESCALATION.md contract with explicit lists of things the watchdog will NEVER do autonomously is a strong safety design — hard-coding the 'never' list prevents scope creep in the autonomous loop
The evaluator contract (eval-contract.md) with explicit score format, parse contract, and status enum is the right abstraction for a quality gate that must be machine-readable
